RULING:
DEFENDANT BORGWARNER MORSE TEC LLC, AS SUCCESSOR-BY-MERGER TO BORG-WARNER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE PUNITIVE DAMAGES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant BorgWarner Morse TEC Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibits B and E are sustained and its objections to plaintiffs' Exhibits C, D, F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, N and O are overruled. Order signed in open court.
